steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best emphasizes how Wilkerson documents the Great Migration. The key note is explicit: "To document this period, Wilkerson narrates the personal journeys of Ida Mae Gladney and Robert Pershing Foster, among others." The correct answer must name both the documentation method (narrating individual journeys) and connect it to the Great Migration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The personal journey of Ida Mae Gladney is featured in the award-winning book *The Warmth of Other Suns*.: Mentions one individual but frames her only as a featured subject — it says nothing about narrating journeys as Wilkerson's method of documenting the Great Migration. The emphasis lands on the award, not the method. ✗
  • Choice B — Isabel Wilkerson chronicles the complex history of the Great Migration in personal detail by narrating the journeys of individuals, such as Ida Mae Gladney and Robert Pershing Foster.: Directly names the method (narrating individual journeys), links it to the subject (the Great Migration), and names the specific individuals from the notes. This is exactly what the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice C — Milwaukee, Wisconsin, is one of the cities whose African American populations grew during the Great Migration.: Background context about a city — Wilkerson's documentation method is never mentioned. Completely off-topic for the question's goal. ✗
  • Choice D — In 2011, Isabel Wilkerson's book *The Warmth of Other Suns* won not only the Mark Lynton History Prize but also the Hurston/Wright Legacy Award.: Focuses entirely on awards — says nothing about how Wilkerson documents the Great Migration. ✗
B is correct.
